query: South Korea arms export deals 2026, especially Poland K2 tank second executive contract, any deals expected July-August 2026 worth over $5 billion
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of the key findings:

## Summary

**Most important correction:** The Poland K2 "second executive contract" (~$6.5 billion) was signed on **August 1, 2025** — not 2026. The research question's premise appears to have an outdated or shifted date.

**On the July–August 2026 >$5 billion premise:** As of the July 12, 2026 horizon, I found **no confirmed new South Korea arms deal worth over $5 billion announced as signed or firmly scheduled for July–August 2026.** The K2 framework's 3rd executive contract is structurally pending (the ~1,000-tank framework is planned across 6 production contracts), but it had no confirmed date or value.

**Key 2026 developments I documented:**
- **April 2026:** Hyundai Rotem–Bumar-Łabędy K2PL local-production cooperation agreement (industrial pact, not a purchase)
- **July 3, 2026:** First K9PL howitzers shipped to Poland (2nd K9 contract execution)
- **Dec 29, 2025:** ~$4B Chunmoo/Homar-K 3rd executive contract
- **June 3, 2026:** ~$700–713M Philippines FA-50 deal (largest confirmed 2026 export at that point)
- Middle East Cheongung-II/M-SAM II contracts totaling ~$6.2B (Saudi $3.2B, Iraq $2.6B, UAE), with Gulf states pressing for faster deliveries post-Iran conflict
- 2025 total exports: $15.4B; Big Four backlog >$72B

The full structured findings with all data points and sources have been submitted.

sources.title: Hyundai Rotem signs $6.5 billion agreement with Poland for second batch of K2 main battle tanks
sources.snippet: Signing took place Friday 1 August at Bumar-Łabędy plant in Gliwice; 116 K2GF delivered 2026–2027, 64 K2PL 2028–2030.

sources.title: With $6.7 billion in new tanks and vehicles, it's 'Armor Week' in Poland
sources.snippet: Warsaw signed almost $6.7 billion in ground vehicle contracts, mostly the $6.5 billion Hyundai Rotem K2 deal signed Friday morning.

sources.title: Poland orders more K2 MBTs, support vehicles
sources.snippet: Polish Armaments Agency signed the second executive contract with Hyundai Rotem for 180 more K2 MBTs at Bumar-Łabędy on 1 August.

sources.title: Poland doubles down on South Korean tanks with $6.5 billion deal
sources.snippet: Poland ordered 180 K2 tanks with 81 accompanying vehicles from Hyundai Rotem; deal worth around $6.5 billion, signed in Gliwice.

sources.title: South Korea's Hyundai Rotem concludes deliveries of 180 K2 main battle tanks to Poland
sources.snippet: First executive contract (Aug 2022) valued at $3.7 billion; second deal (Aug 2025) covers 116 K2GF (2026–27) plus K2PL.

sources.title: Hyundai Rotem signs local production pact for K2 tanks in Poland
sources.snippet: April 28 2026: Hyundai Rotem signed contract with Bumar-Łabędy to locally manufacture the K2PL variant; part of second implementation phase.

sources.title: South Korean tanks for Poland (Defence24 factory report)
sources.snippet: Total of 1,000 tanks ordered across 6 production contracts (excluding engineering vehicles); first contract 2022, all 180 K2s transferred by 2025.

sources.title: First customized K9PL howitzers leave South Korea for Poland under second major artillery contract
sources.snippet: Hanwha Aerospace shipped first K9PL howitzers July 3, 2026; second executive contract signed December 1, 2023 for 152 vehicles.

sources.title: Hanwha Aerospace signs contract to produce Chunmoo rounds in Poland
sources.snippet: ~$4 billion contract signed 29 December 2025 with Polish Armament Agency for Chunmoo CGR-080 guided missiles; third Homar-K executive agreement.

sources.title: Korea's Defense Exports Hit $15.4 Billion in 2025, Set to Grow Further
sources.snippet: DAPA: defense exports reached $15.4 billion in 2025; 'this year will be even better.'

sources.title: Korea Defense Industry 2026: The $37B K-Arms Export Boom
sources.snippet: Big Four firms hold combined backlog exceeding $72 billion (~5 years visibility); Norway $922M Chunmoo Feb 2026; Saudi/Iraq/UAE Cheongung-II.

sources.title: Philippines Signs $710M Deal for 12 More FA-50 Jets from South Korea
sources.snippet: $712.83 million KAI contract for 12 FA-50 signed in Manila June 3; largest South Korean defense export so far this year per DAPA.

sources.title: LIG Nex1 inks $3.2 bn missile defense deal with Saudi Arabia
sources.snippet: LIG Nex1 clinched $3.2 billion deal to export Cheongung-II (M-SAM II) interceptor to Saudi Arabia.

sources.title: South Korean air defense stocks surge on Middle East demand
sources.snippet: South Korean firms completed ~9.5 trillion won (~$6.2 billion) in Cheongung-II contracts to Middle East (UAE, Saudi, Iraq); Gulf pressing for faster deliveries after Iran conflict.

sources.title: S.Korea's LIG Nex1, Iraq set for $2.6 billion deal on M-SAM missile system
sources.snippet: Iraq to import Cheongung-II in a deal estimated at $2.6 billion.

sources.title: South Korea's Hanwha Aerospace delivers more than 200 K9 howitzers to Poland
sources.snippet: First K9 contract Aug 2022 $2.4B for 212 units; December 2023 second contract worth $2.6 billion for 152 units including K9PL.

key_facts: Poland's K2 tank SECOND executive contract (~$6.5 billion, 180 tanks + 81 support vehicles) was signed August 1, 2025 at Bumar-Łabędy, Gliwice — not in 2026.
As of July 12, 2026, no confirmed new South Korea arms deal worth over $5 billion had been announced as signed or firmly scheduled for July–August 2026.
Poland's July 2022 framework for up to ~1,000 K2 tanks is planned to be executed via 6 production contracts; a third K2 executive contract remains pending but undated/unvalued.
First K2 executive contract: Aug 2022, ~$3.4–3.7 billion, 180 K2GF tanks — all delivered by 2025.
April 27–28, 2026: Hyundai Rotem signed a K2PL local-production/maintenance cooperation agreement with Poland's Bumar-Łabędy (industrial pact, not a new government purchase).
K9 howitzer 2nd executive contract (Dec 1, 2023, $2.6B, 152 units) entered execution — first K9PL howitzers shipped July 3, 2026.
Chunmoo/Homar-K 3rd executive contract signed Dec 29, 2025, ~$4 billion for CGR-080 guided missiles.
South Korea's 2025 defense exports reached $15.4 billion (DAPA); officials expect 2026 to be higher.
South Korea's 'Big Four' defense firms hold a combined order backlog exceeding $72 billion (~5 years production visibility).
Philippines signed ~$700–713 million for 12 additional FA-50PH jets on June 3, 2026 — largest confirmed 2026 export at that time.
Combined Middle East Cheongung-II/M-SAM II contracts (Saudi Arabia $3.2B, Iraq ~$2.6B, UAE) total ~$6.2 billion; Gulf states pressing for faster deliveries after Iran conflict (June 2026).
Saudi Arabia Cheongung-II deal: $3.2 billion for ~10 systems (LIG Nex1).
